name: Chernobyl - April 26, 1986
description: RBMK-1000 Unit 4 disaster. During a turbine rundown test, operators reduced power but it unexpectedly collapsed to 30 MWt. Fighting xenon poisoning, they withdrew nearly all control rods — ORM fell to 6-8 rods (minimum 15 required). When coolant flow dropped during the test, the positive void coefficient caused a power surge. AZ-5 was pressed but the graphite displacer tips triggered a further spike. Steam jammed the rods at ~2m of 7m travel. Prompt supercritical excursion destroyed the reactor.

events.message: 25 APR 23:10 — Power reduction resumes from 1,600 MWt (50%)
Grid controller delayed test 9 hours. Xe-135 building in fuel...
26 APR 00:28 — Power transferred to local auto control
Power COLLAPSES to 30 MWt (~1%)! Setpoint error.
Near-zero power. Xe-135 poisoning accelerating rapidly.
Rods deep in core. Xenon trap closing on the reactor.
Operators withdraw rods to recover power against xenon...
01:00 — Stabilized at 200 MWt (6%). ORM already violated.
AUTO CONTROL DISABLED — Manual rod withdrawal
Withdrawing rods to overcome xenon poisoning...
VIOLATION: Rods withdrawn past operating limits!
01:19 — Extra feedwater pumps ON. Coolant flow increased.
Increased coolant flow suppresses boiling temporarily
01:22:30 — ORM at 6–8 rods. Minimum required: 15. (INSAG-7)
01:23:04 — TURBINE TEST BEGINS. Feed valves closed.
Turbine coasting down. Coolant pumps losing power...
Coolant flow dropping! Steam forming in pressure channels.
COOLANT FLOW CRITICAL — Void coefficient feedback starting!
01:23:30 — Power rising. Positive void coefficient active.
01:23:40 — Akimov orders AZ-5. Emergency scram!
AZ-5 PRESSED — Graphite displacer tips enter core first!
POSITIVE SCRAM: Tips displace water → reactivity spike!
01:23:43 — Power exceeds 530 MWt. Fuel rods fracturing!
Rods jammed at ~2–2.5m of 7m. Steam lifting assemblies!
01:23:47 — PROMPT SUPERCRITICAL — Power reaches ~30,000 MWt
Reactor 4 destroyed. Two explosions. 26 April 1986, 01:23:47.
